items.title: Korea’s biggest manufacturers back Config, the TSMC of robot data
items.summary: Samsung, Hyundai and LG just bet on the startup that wants to be robotics' data backbone.

items.title: How to engineer robot tracks and 7th axis systems for real-world environments
items.summary: Experts from Güdel will focus on how harsh and dirty environments impact robot tracks and long-axis linear motion systems. The post How to engineer robot tracks and 7th axis systems for real-world environments appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Cognex releases fully integrated AI-powered vision system for robotics
items.summary: The system combines Cognex-developed edge AI, advanced AI, and rule-based vision tools with a high-performance embedded compute. The post Cognex releases fully integrated AI-powered vision system for robotics appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Why traditional robotics data collection is obsolete and what replaces it
items.summary: Eric Chan discusses Rhoda AI's innovative approach to robotics using video data for scalable, efficient learning. The post Why traditional robotics data collection is obsolete and what replaces it appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Learn how to successfully design hospital logistics robots at the Robotics Summit
items.summary: Experts from Rovex Technologies, SKA Robotics, and ST Engineering Aethon will discuss design considerations for hospital robotics. The post Learn how to successfully design hospital logistics robots at the Robotics Summit appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: 123D: Unifying Multi-Modal Autonomous Driving Data at Scale
items.summary: The pursuit of autonomous driving has produced one of the richest sensor data collections in all of robotics. However, its scale and diversity remain largely untapped. Each dataset adopts different 2D and 3D modalities, such as cameras, lidar, ego states, annotations, traffic lights, and HD maps, with different rates and synchronization schemes.

items.title: 6D Pose Estimation via Keypoint Heatmap Regression with RGB-D Residual Neural Networks
items.summary: In this paper, we propose a modular framework for 6D pose estimation based on keypoint heatmap regression. Our approach combines YOLOv10m for object detection with a ResNet18-based network that predicts 2D heatmaps from RGB images. Keypoints extracted from these heatmaps are used to estimate the 6D object pose via the PnP RANSAC algorithm.

items.title: Nyobolt raises funding to bring fast charging to more robots
items.summary: Nyobolt has grown its revenue fivefold year over year as its high-efficiency power systems enable greater robot performance. The post Nyobolt raises funding to bring fast charging to more robots appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Active Embodiment Identification with Reinforcement Learning for Legged Robots
items.summary: We present an active embodiment identification method for legged robots that jointly learns information-seeking behavior and explicit embodiment prediction. Using a history-augmented URMA architecture, the method infers joint-level and global embodiment parameters through interaction with the environment in simulation across different morphologies.

items.title: Evaluation of an Actuated Spine in Agile Quadruped Locomotion
items.summary: The spine plays a crucial role in the dynamic locomotion of quadrupedal animals, improving the stability, speed, and efficiency of their gait, especially for fast-paced and highly agile movements. Therefore, the spine is also a promising and natural way to extend the capabilities of quadruped robots. This paper empirically investigates the benefits of an actuated spine for learning agile quadruped locomotion.

items.title: How coupling stiffness and misalignment compensation impact servo performance
items.summary: Sponsored by GAM. Servo couplings connect components while compensating for misalignment, providing the torsional stiffness necessary for precise, responsive motion control performance. The post How coupling stiffness and misalignment compensation impact servo performance appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: TAVIS: A Benchmark for Egocentric Active Vision and Anticipatory Gaze in Imitation Learning
items.summary: Active vision -- where a policy controls its own gaze during manipulation -- has emerged as a key capability for imitation learning, with multiple independent systems demonstrating its benefits in the past year. Yet there is no shared benchmark to compare approaches or quantify what active vision contributes, on which task types, and under what conditions.

items.title: One World, Dual Timeline: Decoupled Spatio-Temporal Gaussian Scene Graph for 4D Cooperative Driving Reconstruction
items.summary: Reconstructing dynamic scenes from Vehicle-to-Infrastructure Cooperative Autonomous Driving (VICAD) data is fundamentally complicated by temporal asynchrony: vehicle and infrastructure cameras operate on independent clocks, capturing the same dynamic agent such as cars and pedestrians at different physical times.

items.title: AERO-VIS: Asynchronous Event-based Real-time Onboard Visual-Inertial SLAM
items.summary: The robustness of event cameras to high dynamic range and motion blur holds the potential to improve visual odometry systems in challenging environments. Although their high temporal resolution does not require synchronous processing, most event-based odometry methods still run at fixed rates, which simplifies system design but restricts latency and throughput.

items.title: Melding LLM and temporal logic for reliable human-swarm collaboration in complex scenarios
items.summary: Robot swarms promise scalable assistance in complex and hazardous environments. Task planning lies at the core of human-swarm collaboration, translating the operator's intent into coordinated swarm actions and helping determine when validation or intervention is required during execution.

items.title: Many-to-Many Multi-Agent Pickup and Delivery
items.summary: Multi-robot systems in automated warehouses must manage continuous streams of pickup-and-delivery tasks while ensuring efficiency and safety. Prior work on Multi-Agent Pickup-and-Delivery (MAPD) has largely focused on the one-to-one variant, where each task has a fixed pickup and delivery location.

items.title: Text-to-CAD Evaluation with CADTests
items.summary: Text-to-CAD has recently emerged as an important task with the potential to substantially accelerate design workflows. Despite its significance, there has been surprisingly little work on Text-to-CAD evaluation, and assessing CAD model generation performance remains a considerable challenge. In this work, we introduce a new evaluation perspective for Text-to-CAD based on automated testing.

items.title: NoiseGate: Learning Per-Latent Timestep Schedules as Information Gating in World Action Models
items.summary: World Action Models (WAMs) are an emerging family of policies that tie robot action generation to future-observation modeling. In this work, we focus on the joint video--action modeling paradigm, where actions and imagined future observations are co-generated along a shared denoising or flow trajectory, so that perception, prediction, and control are coupled within one generative process.

items.title: Sensitivity-Based Robust NMPC for Close-Proximity Offshore Wind Turbine Inspection with a Tilted Multirotor
items.summary: Close-proximity offshore wind turbine inspection requires strict clearance control around large cylindrical structures under wind and model mismatch. Nominal Nonlinear Model Predictive Control (NMPC) may violate safety constraints when mass, inertia, thrust effectiveness, drag, or wind conditions differ from nominal assumptions.

items.title: CommandSwarm: Safety-Aware Natural Language-to-Behavior-Tree Generation for Robotic Swarms
items.summary: Natural-language interfaces can make swarm robotics more accessible to non-expert operators, but they must translate ambiguous user intent into executable swarm behaviors without unsupported actions, malformed programs, or unsafe plans. This paper presents CommandSwarm, a safety-aware language-to-behavior-tree pipeline for generating XML behavior trees (BTs) from speech or text commands.

items.title: Offline-Online Hierarchical 3D Global Relocalization With Synthetic LiDAR Sensing and Descriptor-Space Retrieval
items.summary: 3D global relocalization is one of the key capabilities for mobile robots in practical applications. However, in large scale spaces, existing methods often suffer from prolonged online relocalization time due to factors such as the massive pose search space and high computational overhead. To address these issues, this paper proposes an offline-online hierarchical framework that decouples the search space.

items.title: Drifting Field Policy: A One-Step Generative Policy via Wasserstein Gradient Flow
items.summary: We propose Drifting Field Policy (DFP), a non-ODE one-step generative policy built on the drifting model paradigm. We frame the policy update as a reverse-KL Wasserstein-2 gradient flow toward a soft target policy, so that each DFP update corresponds to a gradient step in probability space.

items.title: Finite-Time Analysis of MCTS in Continuous POMDP Planning
items.summary: This paper presents a finite-time analysis for Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) in Partially Observable Markov Decision Processes (POMDPs), with probabilistic concentration bounds in both discrete and continuous observation spaces.

items.title: PhySPRING: Structure-Preserving Reduction of Physics-Informed Twins via GNN
items.summary: Physics-based digital twins aim to predict the dynamics of real-world objects under interaction, enabling real-to-sim-to-real applications in robotics.

items.title: FactoryBench: Evaluating Industrial Machine Understanding
items.summary: We introduce FactoryBench, a benchmark for evaluating time-series models and LLMs on machine understanding over industrial robotic telemetry.

items.title: QNX to bring hands-on demonstrations and new research to the Robotics Summit
items.summary: QNX encourages visitors to stop by Booth 307 at the Robotics Summit & Expo to see its demonstrations firsthand. The post QNX to bring hands-on demonstrations and new research to the Robotics Summit appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Electric Axle and Wheel Module Driveline Concepts for Self-propelled Agricultural Machinery and Equipment Carriers
items.summary: Direct electric drivelines without power-split open new design freedom for frame and suspension design, along with often lower energy losses. This paper focuses on self-propelled agricultural machinery (combine and forage harvest-ers, root crop harvesters), equipment carriers, propelled trailers and field robots.

items.title: Hugging Face launches agentic toolkit for Reachy Mini
items.summary: Users describe the behavior they'd like to see in plain English, and the agent writes, tests, and ships the code to Reachy Mini. The post Hugging Face launches agentic toolkit for Reachy Mini appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Proactive Instance Navigation with Comparative Judgment for Ambiguous User Queries
items.summary: Natural-language instance navigation becomes challenging when the initial user request does not uniquely specify the target instance. A practical agent should reduce the user's burden by actively asking only the information needed to distinguish the target from similar distractors, rather than requiring a detailed description upfront.

items.title: Podcast: Colin Angle discusses building companion robots with Familiar Machines & Magic
items.summary: iRobot co-founder Colin Angle talks about his latest venture, creating next-gen social robots with Familiar Machines. The post Podcast: Colin Angle discusses building companion robots with Familiar Machines & Magic appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Brightpick to outline the path to lights-out warehouses at Robotics Summit
items.summary: Jan Zizka, co-founder and CEO of Brightpick, will outline a practical roadmap to more autonomous warehouse operations. The post Brightpick to outline the path to lights-out warehouses at Robotics Summit appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Kirigami-Structured Electronic Capsule for Long-Term Continuous Gastric Monitoring
items.summary: Ingestible electronic systems enable non-invasive, in situ sensing within the gastrointestinal (GI) tract, yet clinical translation has been limited by uncontrolled transit, short operational lifetimes, and unreliable wireless communication that prevent continuous monitoring.

items.title: Johnson & Johnson completes clinical study for OTTAVA robotic surgical system
items.summary: Johnson & Johnson said it has demonstrated the feasibility of OTTAVA's compact design for small operating rooms. The post Johnson & Johnson completes clinical study for OTTAVA robotic surgical system appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: LineRides: Line-Guided Reinforcement Learning for Bicycle Robot Stunts
items.summary: Designing reward functions for agile robotic maneuvers in reinforcement learning remains difficult, and demonstration-based approaches often require reference motions that are unavailable for novel platforms or extreme stunts.

items.title: 1X begins production of NEO humanoids in California facility
items.summary: 1X has launched full-scale production at its 'NEO factory,' bringing vertically integrated humanoid robot manufacturing to the U.S. The post 1X begins production of NEO humanoids in California facility appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Genesis AI introduces GENE-26.5 model for more dexterous robot manipulation
items.summary: Genesis AI says its GENE-26.5 foundation model uses an advanced data engine and a proprietary robotic hand for new levels of dexterity. The post Genesis AI introduces GENE-26.5 model for more dexterous robot manipulation appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Marc Lore says that AI will soon enable anyone to open a restaurant
items.summary: Wonder wants to turn its robotic kitchens into AI-powered “restaurant factories,” letting anyone spin up a virtual food brand with a prompt.

items.title: WaiV Robotics emerges from stealth to help drones take off and land at sea
items.summary: With $7.5 million in funding, WaiV Robotics is working to productize its drone landing system, and find OEM partners to work with. The post WaiV Robotics emerges from stealth to help drones take off and land at sea appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Tutor Intelligence builds Data Factory to train robot AI in the real world
items.summary: Tutor Intelligence is running 100 Sonny semi-humanoid robots in its headquarters while sharing technology and data with its Cassie mobile manipulator. The post Tutor Intelligence builds Data Factory to train robot AI in the real world appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Separation Assurance between Heterogeneous Fleets of Small Unmanned Aerial Systems via Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: In the envisioned future dense urban airspace, multiple companies will operate heterogeneous fleets of small unmanned aerial systems (sUASs), where each fleet includes several homogeneous aircraft with identical policies and configurations, e.g., equipage, sensing, and communication ranges, making tactical deconfliction highly complex for the aircraft.

items.title: 3D Generation for Embodied AI and Robotic Simulation: A Survey
items.summary: Embodied AI and robotic systems increasingly depend on scalable, diverse, and physically grounded 3D content for simulation-based training and real-world deployment.

items.title: Into the Omniverse: Manufacturing’s Simulation-First Era Has Arrived
items.summary: Manufacturing’s traditional design-build-test cycle rested on a single assumption: Real-world testing was the only reliable test environment.

items.title: NVIDIA and Partners Showcase the Future of AI-Driven Manufacturing at Hannover Messe 2026
items.summary: Manufacturing is at an inflection point. Across every major industrial economy, the pressure to do more with less — due to faster design cycles, leaner operations and strain on skilled labor pools — is accelerating the shift to AI-driven production. The question is no longer whether to adopt AI, but how fast and at what […]

items.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
items.summary: This National Robotics Week, NVIDIA is highlighting the breakthroughs that are bringing AI into the physical world — as well as the growing wave of robots transforming industries, from agricultural and manufacturing to energy and beyond. Advancements in robot learning, simulation and foundation models are accelerating development, enabling robots to move from training in virtual […]

items.title: Into the Omniverse: NVIDIA GTC Showcases Virtual Worlds Powering the Physical AI Era
items.summary: Editor’s note: This post is part of Into the Omniverse, a series focused on how developers, 3D practitioners, and enterprises can transform their workflows using the latest advances in OpenUSD and NVIDIA Omniverse. NVIDIA GTC last week showcased a turning point in physical AI: Robots, vehicles and factories are scaling from single use cases and […]

items.title: NVIDIA GTC 2026: Live Updates on What’s Next in AI
items.summary: Rolling coverage from San Jose, including NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang’s keynote, news highlights, live demos and on‑the‑ground color through March 19.

items.title: From Simulation to Production: How to Build Robots With AI
items.summary: The latest open models and frameworks from NVIDIA bring together simulation, robot learning and embedded compute to accelerate cloud-to-robot workflows.

items.title: Gecko Robotics lands the largest US Navy robotics deal yet
items.summary: Gecko Robotics inked a five-year deal to help the U.S. Navy monitor and predict needed maintenance on its fleet of ships.

items.title: As Open Models Spark AI Boom, NVIDIA Jetson Brings It to Life at the Edge
items.summary: The Cat 306 CR mini-excavator weighs just under eight tons and fits inside a standard shipping container. It’s the machine a contractor rents when the job site is tight: a utility trench near a foundation, a basement dig in a dense neighborhood. The cab is roughly the size of a phone booth. The operator sits […]

items.title: Qualcomm’s partnership with Neura Robotics is just the beginning
items.summary: Neura Robotics is going to build new robots on top of Qualcomm's new IQ10 processors that were released at CES.

items.title: ABB Robotics Taps NVIDIA Omniverse to Deliver Industrial‑Grade Physical AI at Scale
items.summary: ABB Robotics and NVIDIA today announced a breakthrough partnership that brings industrial‑grade physical AI to the factory floor. By integrating NVIDIA Omniverse libraries directly into its RobotStudio programming and simulation suite, ABB Robotics will now deliver physically accurate simulation capabilities in its platform, dramatically cutting engineering time, reducing deployment costs by up to 40% and […]

items.title: Docking and Persistent Operations for a Resident Underwater Vehicle
items.summary: Our understanding of the oceans remains limited by sparse and infrequent observations, primarily because current methods are constrained by the high cost and logistical effort of underwater monitoring, relying either on sporadic surveys across broad areas or on long-term measurements at fixed locations.

items.title: NVIDIA and Global Industrial Software Leaders Partner With India’s Largest Manufacturers to Drive AI Boom
items.summary: India is entering a new age of industrialization, as AI transforms how the world designs, builds and runs physical products and systems. The country is investing $134 billion dollars in new manufacturing capacity across construction, automotive, renewable energy and robotics, creating both a massive challenge and opportunity to build software-defined factories from day one. At […]

items.title: HAIC: Humanoid Agile Object Interaction Control via Dynamics-Aware World Model
items.summary: Humanoid robots show promise for complex whole-body tasks in unstructured environments. Although Human-Object Interaction (HOI) has advanced, most methods focus on fully actuated objects rigidly coupled to the robot, ignoring underactuated objects with independent dynamics and non-holonomic constraints. These introduce control challenges from coupling forces and occlusions.

items.title: Upside Robotics is reducing fertilizer use and waste in corn crops
items.summary: Upside Robotics builds autonomous solar-powered robots that can help farmers reduce their fertilizer use by 70%.

items.title: Into the Omniverse: Physical AI Open Models and Frameworks Advance Robots and Autonomous Systems
items.summary: Open source has become essential for driving innovation in robotics and autonomy. By providing access to critical infrastructure — from simulation frameworks to AI models — NVIDIA is enabling collaborative development that accelerates the path to safer, more capable autonomous systems.

items.title: How YC-backed Bucket Robotics survived its first CES
items.summary: Now, the startup is turning its attention to building the business, fundraising, and striking commercial deals.

items.title: Oshen built the first ocean robot to collect data in a Category 5 hurricane
items.summary: Oshen has signed contracts with multiple government agencies for its C-Star robots to collect ocean data autonomously.

items.title: AI’s Next Revolution: Multiply Labs Is Scaling Robotics-Driven Cell Therapy Biomanufacturing Labs
items.summary: Startup works with leading cell therapy companies to bring robotics manufacturing into the clean room, reducing costs by more than 70% while accelerating output compared with legacy systems.

items.title: Inside CES 2026’s “physical AI” takeover
items.summary: After years of chatbots and image generators, AI is finally leaving the screen. At CES 2026, that shift became impossible to ignore.  The annual tech showcase in Las Vegas was dominated by “physical AI” and robotics, from Boston Dynamic’s newly redesigned Atlas humanoid robot to AI-powered ice makers (yes, really). The companies in attendance clearly want consumers […]

items.title: Japan Science and Technology Agency Develops NVIDIA-Powered Moonshot Robot for Elderly Care
items.summary: The next universal technology since the smartphone is on the horizon — and it may be a little less pocket friendly. The Moonshot research program, funded by the Japan Science and Technology Agency and accelerated by NVIDIA AI and robotics technologies, is working to create a world by 2050 where AI-powered, autonomously learning robots are […]

items.title: Steel, Sensors and Silicon: How Caterpillar Is Bringing Edge AI to the Jobsite
items.summary: At CES, Caterpillar reveals how it’s integrating NVIDIA technologies, from NVIDIA Jetson Thor to speech models, to transform the world’s heavy industries.

items.title: From Warehouse to Wallet: New State of AI in Retail and CPG Survey Uncovers How AI Is Rewiring Supply Chains and Customer Experiences
items.summary: AI has transformed retail and consumer packaged goods (CPG) operations, enhancing customer analysis and segmentation to enable greater personalization for marketing and advertising, and boosting the speed and accuracy of demand forecasting for supply chains and logistics. Companies are also raising the bar for customer engagement through intelligent digital shopping assistants and catalog enrichment by […]

items.title: NVIDIA Unveils New Open Models, Data and Tools to Advance AI Across Every Industry
items.summary: Expanding the open model universe, NVIDIA today released new open models, data and tools to advance AI across every industry. These models — spanning the NVIDIA Nemotron family for agentic AI, the NVIDIA Cosmos platform for physical AI, the new NVIDIA Alpamayo family for autonomous vehicle development, NVIDIA Isaac GR00T for robotics and NVIDIA Clara […]

items.title: Pickle Robot adds Tesla veteran as first CFO
items.summary: Jeff Evanson recently joined Pickle Robot as the company's first CFO as the startup has allegedly expanded its partnership with UPS.

items.title: Into the Omniverse: OpenUSD and NVIDIA Halos Accelerate Safety for Robotaxis, Physical AI Systems
items.summary: Physical AI is moving from research labs into the real world, powering intelligent robots and autonomous vehicles (AVs) — such as robotaxis — that must reliably sense, reason and act amid unpredictable conditions.

items.title: Cheers to AI: ADAM Robot Bartender Makes Drinks at Vegas Golden Knights Game
items.summary: In Las Vegas’s T-Mobile Arena, fans of the Golden Knights are getting more than just hockey — they’re getting a taste of the future. ADAM, a robot developed with NVIDIA Isaac libraries, is pouring drinks and turning heads in one of the NHL’s most exciting venues. ADAM, short for Automated Dual Arm Mixologist, was developed […]

items.title: 1X struck a deal to send its ‘home’ humanoids to factories and warehouses
items.summary: Despite launching as a humanoid robot designed to help consumers around the house, 1X's Neo robots are heading to industrial use cases.

items.title: Robots’ Holiday Wishes Come True: NVIDIA Jetson Platform Offers High-Performance Edge AI at Festive Prices
items.summary: Editor’s note: This blog has been updated to showcase additional recent innovations tapping into the NVIDIA Jetson platform. Developers, researchers, hobbyists and students can take a byte out of holiday shopping this season as NVIDIA has unwrapped special discounts on the NVIDIA Jetson family of developer kits for edge AI and robotics — available through […]

items.title: This Khosla-backed startup can track drones, trucks, and robotaxis, inch by inch
items.summary: Point One Navigation, now valued at $230 million, is building out well beyond automotive.

items.title: Why a researcher is building robots that look and act like bats
items.summary: These palm-sized robots use ultrasound signals to navigate harsh conditions in search and rescue missions.

items.title: AI researchers ’embodied’ an LLM into a robot – and it started channeling Robin Williams
items.summary: AI researchers at Andon Labs embedded various LLMs in a vacuum robot to test how ready they were to be embodied. And hilarity ensued.

items.title: Coco Robotics taps UCLA professor to lead new physical AI research lab
items.summary: Coco Robotics is working toward automating its fleet of delivery robots using its millions of miles of collected data.

items.title: The world is just not quite ready for humanoids yet
items.summary: Despite the amount of money being injected into the industry, humanoids won't be able to learn dexterity -- or the fine motor movements with hands -- rendering them essentially useless.

items.title: GATO: GPU-Accelerated and Batched Trajectory Optimization for Scalable Edge Model Predictive Control
items.summary: While Model Predictive Control (MPC) delivers strong performance across robotics applications, solving the underlying (batches of) nonlinear trajectory optimization (TO) problems online remains computationally demanding. Existing GPU-accelerated approaches either parallelize single solves, handle large batches at sub-real-time rates, or sacrifice model generality for speed.

items.title: SoftBank bulks up its robotics portfolio with ABB Group’s robotics unit
items.summary: SoftBank says this acquisition will help it dive deeper into robotics as it considers physical AI to be the next frontier.

items.title: TAG-K: Tail-Averaged Greedy Kaczmarz for Computationally Efficient and Performant Online Inertial Parameter Estimation
items.summary: Accurate online inertial parameter estimation is essential for adaptive robotic control, enabling real-time adjustment to payload changes, environmental interactions, and system wear. Traditional methods often struggle to track abrupt parameter shifts or incur high computational costs, limiting their effectiveness in dynamic environments and for computationally constrained robotic systems.

items.title: Geometric Analysis of Neural Regression Collapse via Intrinsic Dimension
items.summary: Neural multivariate regression underpins a wide range of domains, including control, robotics, and finance, yet the geometry of its learned representations remains poorly characterized. While neural collapse has been shown to benefit generalization in classification, we find that analogous collapse in regression consistently degrades performance.

items.title: Former OpenAI and DeepMind researchers raise whopping $300M seed to automate science
items.summary: Periodic Labs has raised from a tech industry who's who, including Andreessen Horowitz, Nvidia, Elad Gil, Jeff Dean, Eric Schmidt, and Jeff Bezos.

items.title: Live demo fails, AI safety wins, and the golden age of robotics
items.summary: This week on Equity, Anthony Ha, Kirsten Korosec, and Max Zeff unpack the biggest moves in AI, robotics, and regulation. Listen to the full episode to hear about: Equity will be back next week. Subscribe wherever you get your podcasts! Equity is TechCrunch’s flagship podcast, produced by Theresa Loconsolo, and posts every Wednesday and Friday. […]

items.title: Why Runway is eyeing the robotics industry for future revenue growth
items.summary: Runway is building up a robotics-focused team and fine-tuning its existing models for robotics and self-driving car customers.

items.title: Pushing Blocks without Fixed Walls via Checkable Gizmos: Push-1 is PSPACE-Complete
items.summary: We prove PSPACE-completeness of Push-1: given a rectangular grid of 1 x 1 cells, each possibly occupied by a movable block, can a robot move from one specified location to another, given the ability to push up to one block at a time? In particular, we remove the need for fixed (immovable) walls from a 2022 result. This fundamental model of block pushing, introduced in 1999, abstracts the mechanics of many video games.

items.title: SCOUT: Closed-Loop in-vivo System for Continuous Methane Concentration Monitoring in Cattle
items.summary: Enteric methane measurement from ruminant livestock faces fundamental trade-offs between accuracy and operational feasibility. Existing methods quantify methane after eructation and atmospheric dilution, limiting temporal resolution and confounding biological signals with environmental variables.

items.title: Tesla is reportedly behind on its pledge to build 5,000 Optimus bots this year
items.summary: Tesla is behind on its goal to produce at least 5,000 Optimus humanoid robots by the end of 2025.

items.title: Surgical Visual Understanding (SurgVU) Dataset
items.summary: Owing to recent advances in machine learning and the ability to harvest large amounts of data during robotic-assisted surgeries, surgical data science is ripe for foundational work. We present a large dataset of surgical videos and their accompanying labels for this purpose. We describe how the data was collected and some of its unique attributes. Multiple example problems are outlined.

items.title: UNCOM: Zero-shot Context-Aware Command Understanding for Tabletop Scenarios
items.summary: This paper presents UNCOM, a novel hybrid framework for interpreting natural human commands in tabletop scenarios. The system integrates multiple sources of information -- speech, gestures, and scene context -- to extract structured, actionable instructions for robots.

items.title: Goal-Conditioned Decision Transformer for Multi-Goal Offline Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) in robotics faces significant hurdles regarding sample efficiency and generalization across varying goals. While Offline RL mitigates the need for costly online interactions, its integration with goal-conditioned policies and transformer-based architectures remains underexplored. We introduce a Goal-Conditioned Decision Transformer adapted for offline multi-goal robotics.

items.title: Code Generation and Conic Constraints for Model-Predictive Control on Microcontrollers with Conic-TinyMPC
items.summary: Model-predictive control (MPC) is a state-of-the-art control method for constrained robotic systems, yet deployment on resource-limited hardware remains difficult. This challenge is magnified by expressive conic constraints, which offer greater modeling power but require significantly more computation than linear alternatives.
